findings.title: Head-on multi-target scenario is defined but never driven through a tracker with continuity/ID-stability/accuracy assertions
findings.description: CLAUDE.md lists head-on as a required scenario asserting track continuity, ID stability, and accuracy vs truth. The synthetic `head_on` scenario (adapters/benchmark/SimScenarioRun.cpp:131-148: two anti-parallel targets closing at ~50 m/s crossing at only 5 m lateral separation) exists ONLY as a definition — test_sim_scenario_run.cpp:21 asserts the label is present, and the sweep runs it but no test evaluates its tracked output (test_sweep.cpp uses only "tiny_line"; test_bus_regression covers crossing/overtaking/parallel/bearing-only, never head_on). The only other head-on test, tests/integration/test_full_stack_pipeline.cpp, is a SINGLE-target head-on and asserts manager.tracks().size()==1 — it cannot exercise the two-target ID-swap failure mode at all. tests/collision/test_cpa*.cpp reference "head-on" but only test CPA geometry, not the tracker. Searched: grep for head_on/HeadOn/head-on across tests/ (only CPA geometry + the label-count test); test_sweep/test_bus_regression/test_consistency assertion lines.
findings.why_it_matters: Two targets meeting head-on at 5 m separation is the canonical point where the associator swaps the two track_ids. An ID swap keeps mgr.size()==2 and near-identical OSPA, so it violates the stable-track-identity invariant (#5) and mis-attributes collision risk to the wrong vessel while every existing test stays green.
findings.suggested_test: Drive the head_on scenario (or buildCrossingTargetsScenario with the anti-parallel geometry) through the GNN Tracker AND the PMBM/MHT trackers; assert mgr.size()==2 throughout and countIdSwitches(...)==0 (strict, like overtaking), so a swap at the meeting point fails.

verdict.reasoning:
The claim's narrow literal statement — that the *named* `head_on` scenario object in adapters/benchmark/SimScenarioRun.cpp is never scored through a tracker — is technically true (test_sim_scenario_run.cpp:21 only counts its label; the sweep runs it but no test evaluates its tracked output). But the "gap that matters," per the claim's own why_it_matters, is the failure mode: two anti-parallel targets meeting head-on at close separation where the associator swaps the two track_ids, staying green because count stays 2 and OSPA stays low. That failure mode IS covered, so the claim is refuted.

REFUTING TEST: tests/scenario/test_crossing.cpp:16, `TEST(Stress, CrossingTargetsStayCountedAndIdsMostlyStable)`. It builds two targets via the SAME builder the head_on scenario uses — buildCrossingTargetsScenario — with anti-parallel velocities (25,0) and (-25,0) and lateral offset ±10 m (head_on uses the identical builder/velocities with ±5 m). Confirmed via Builders.cpp: the two targets move in straight lines directly toward and past each other, closing at 50 m/s and passing at 20 m separation at t=20s — this is head-on geometry, not a right-angle crossing (the "crossing" label is a geometric misnomer). It drives the scenario through the full EKF+GNN+CV2D tracker (est, GnnAssociator(50), Tracker gate 30) and asserts BOTH:
  - EXPECT_EQ(mgr.size(), 2u)  — continuity/count
  - EXPECT_LE(countIdSwitches(r.steps, 30.0), 2)  — ID stability

Why it catches the exact breakage the claim raises: countIdSwitches (core/scenario/Metrics.cpp:9) maps each truth target to its nearest track each step and increments when that truth's assigned track_id changes between steps. An associator ID swap at the close crossing (track A jumping to target 2, track B to target 1) flips both nearest-track_ids near CPA, driving switches above the ≤2 bound → red. This is precisely the ID-swap-with-stable-count-and-low-OSPA failure the claim says every test would miss; the OSPA-only bus-regression "Crossing" test would indeed miss it, but this test uses the swap-sensitive metric.

The claim's search grepped only for head_on/HeadOn/head-on and thus missed this test (named "Crossing" / "Stress"), and it explicitly dismissed the bus-regression crossing as "not head_on" without recognizing that both use anti-parallel head-on velocities. The verifier instructions warn test names often don't match source names — this is that case.

Secondary note: test_sim_multisensor_scenario_run.cpp:110 (HeadonFusionAccuracyGate) also drives a 2-vessel head-on through IMM+MHT asserting id_switches<6, but its fixture geometry passes at ~930 m minimum separation (verified from truth.csv) and is fixture-gated (skips without SIMMS_DIR), so it alone would NOT exercise the swap ambiguity — it is not the load-bearing refutation. test_crossing.cpp is, being unconditional (no fixtures) and at 20 m separation with the swap-sensitive metric.

The 5 m vs 10 m offset difference is not a distinct failure mode: at closing 50 m/s with 30 m tracker gate / 50 m association gate, both put the pair well within mutual gates at CPA, so both fully exercise the association ambiguity.

findings.title: Crossing ID-stability bound (<=2 switches) exactly admits a full two-target ID swap
findings.description: test_crossing asserts EXPECT_LE(countIdSwitches(r.steps,30.0),2) for the two crossing targets. countIdSwitches (core/scenario/Metrics.cpp) counts, per truth index, transitions of the nearest track_id from one nonzero value to a different nonzero value. A clean full ID swap of the two crossing targets produces exactly ONE counted switch per truth target = 2 total, which passes EXPECT_LE(...,2). So the assertion tolerates the precise failure (a crossing-point ID swap) it is meant to guard. Contrast test_overtaking.cpp:34 which uses ==0 (strict). mgr.size()==2 also survives a swap, so nothing in this test distinguishes clean tracking from a complete swap.
findings.why_it_matters: A regression that swaps the two IDs at the crossing point violates ID stability (#5) yet leaves this required-scenario test green, because the tolerance is set exactly at the swap count.
findings.suggested_test: Tighten to countIdSwitches==0, or add an assertion that each truth target maps to a single track_id for the entire post-confirmation span (no swap), separating a swap (2) from incidental settling.

findings.title: PMBM determinism is asserted only on two aggregate scalars, not on per-step track IDs/positions
findings.description: PmbmScenario.ReplayDeterminism runs the same scenario twice and asserts only EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ(mean_ospa) and EXPECT_EQ(final_track_count). The comment claims output must be "bit-identical" but the test never compares per-step track_ids or positions. The bench determinism test (tests/benchmark/test_bench_determinism.cpp:36) hashes all metric rows for ONLY configs.front(), which is imm_cv_ct_mht (Config.cpp:476-483) — so it covers the MHT path, not PMBM (imm_cv_ct_pmbm, Config.cpp:589). Thus the headline PMBM tracker's determinism (invariant #4) is guarded only by two scalars. Searched: all tests/pmbm/*.cpp and tests/benchmark/*determinism* for per-step/id comparison; none for PMBM.
findings.why_it_matters: PMBM uses Murty + std::map ordering; a change that made track_id assignment order or per-scan estimate ordering nondeterministic while preserving mean_ospa and final count (e.g. reshuffled IDs, tie-break drift) would break replay reproducibility for the headline tracker with no test failing.
findings.suggested_test: Extend runPmbm to return the full per-step (id, position) log and assert the two runs are element-wise identical (like reorder_buffer_e2e stepsEqual), or add PMBM to the bench_determinism config set.

findings.title: No end-to-end replay-determinism test through real adapters (NMEA/CSV in -> TrackOutput out, byte-identical twice)
findings.description: The CLAUDE.md requirement is "same log replayed twice -> identical output." test_bus_determinism asserts determinism of the sensor BUS measurement stream only (times/values), not tracker output. bench_determinism (test_bench_determinism.cpp) hashes metric ROWS from synthetic emitters for the MHT config — not raw TrackOutput and not through the real NMEA/CSV parse path. No test feeds a fixed NMEA or CSV log through adapter -> Tracker -> toTrackOutput twice and compares byte-identical TrackOutput (ids, lat/lon, covariance). Searched: tests/replay/*, tests/adapters/*, and *determinism* — the replay adapter tests (test_ais_csv_replay_adapter, test_autoferry_json_replay) assert parse correctness, not two-run output identity.
findings.why_it_matters: Determinism is invariant #4 and the basis for forensic replay. The end-to-end contract as literally stated (log -> identical output) is verified only at the synthetic-metric level for one tracker; a nondeterminism introduced in the adapter->core assembly or TrackOutput derivation would not be caught.
findings.suggested_test: Add a replay test: parse a small fixed NMEA/CSV log twice through the real adapters + one tracker, drain toTrackOutput each step, and assert the two output sequences are byte/field-identical.

findings.title: Non-cooperative (no-AIS, bearing-only) target has no tracked-output continuity/ID assertion
findings.description: The `non_cooperative` scenario (Bearing2D, no AIS) is only definition-tested (test_sim_scenario_run.cpp:26 label present; :158-161 model==Bearing2D). Its tracked output — that a target with no MMSI still gets tracked and kept with a stable id (ADR-0002 presence-over-classification) — is not asserted anywhere on this scenario. Bearing-only tracking ACCURACY is partially covered for a different single-target geometry by BusRegression.BearingOnlyMovingSensorMeanOspaWithinTolerance (test_bus_regression.cpp:269, OSPA-ratio tolerance only, no id-stability).
findings.why_it_matters: A regression that fails to confirm or that drops/re-IDs a non-cooperative (radar/EO-only) target would leave a real vessel untracked or with unstable identity — the exact degraded mode ADR-0002 forbids — while the sweep row still emits and no assertion trips.
findings.suggested_test: Drive the non_cooperative scenario through a tracker and assert the target is confirmed within bounded latency, kept for the run, and holds one stable track_id (countIdSwitches==0).

units.coverage_notes: WELL COVERED (good news for synthesis): (1) crossing, overtaking, ais_dropout each have dedicated GNN-pipeline scenario tests (tests/scenario/) asserting mgr.size() and countIdSwitches; overtaking is strict (==0) and ais_dropout is strict (size==1, switches==0) — these two are strong. (2) Clock skew is well covered: tests/sim/test_skew_injector.cpp (injector unit incl. identity, determinism-same-seed, truth-timestamp preservation, RemoteTrack index bound) plus tests/scenario/test_reorder_buffer_e2e.cpp which asserts, under skew, determinism (stepsEqual: per-step id+position identical across two runs), monotonic drains, accuracy parity vs unskewed baseline (mean_ospa within tol), and drop counts matching an independently-recomputed ground-truth late count — genuinely strong. (3) MHT-path determinism is solid: test_bench_determinism hashes ALL non-wallclock metric rows across two full sweep runs of configs.front() (which is imm_cv_ct_mht, the canonical MHT — note the in-test comment \"ekf_cv_gnn\" is stale/misleading but harmless). (4) GNN single-hypothesis Tracker determinism is covered by reorder_buffer_e2e stepsEqual. (5) Head-on CPA GEOMETRY is well tested (tests/collision/test_cpa*.cpp). (6) Full-stack head-on single-target integration (test_full_stack_pipeline.cpp) strongly asserts lifecycle sequence, bias convergence, CPA Entered<Exited, and pull/push TrackOutput consistency. (7) Sim bus measurement-stream determinism (same seed identical, diff seed differs) is covered. countIdSwitches is a reasonable per-truth-target id-stability proxy (tracks nearest track_id per truth index over time). COULD NOT DETERMINE (read-only, no build): actual green/red status of any test; whether fixture-gated replay tests (philos/haxr/autoferry) actually run vs skip in this tree; whether the sim head_on/non_cooperative/speed_change scenarios feed any downstream A/B assertion I did not open (I checked test_sweep, test_consistency, test_bus_regression, test_bench_runner — none assert tracked output for head_on/non_cooperative). The five gaps reported are the required scenarios/paths whose tracked-output property is either unasserted (head_on, non_cooperative), asserted too loosely to catch the target failure (crossing swap tolerance), or asserted only at aggregate/measurement level rather than byte-identical output (PMBM determinism, full-adapter replay determinism).

findings.title: Canonical crossing/overtaking tests assert continuity via a degenerate metric and never assert accuracy vs truth
findings.description: test_crossing.cpp (line 33-34) asserts only mgr.size()==2u and countIdSwitches(r.steps,30.0)<=2; test_overtaking.cpp:33-34 asserts mgr.size()==2u and countIdSwitches(...,30.0)==0. r.mean_ospa is computed by runScenario but discarded in both. countIdSwitches (core/scenario/Metrics.cpp) matches each truth to the nearest track ONLY within `cutoff` (30 m); when no track is within 30 m of a truth it sets best_id=0 and counts NO switch and does not update `last[i]`. So the switch count is 0 both when IDs are perfectly stable AND when both tracks have drifted permanently >30 m off truth. Combined with size==2 (which two ghost/clutter tracks also satisfy), an estimator/association/measurement-model regression that keeps two track objects alive but drives them far off truth passes green.
findings.why_it_matters: Crossing and overtaking are two of the CLAUDE.md-mandated scenario tests whose stated purpose is asserting continuity, ID stability AND accuracy vs truth. Accuracy is not asserted at all here, and the continuity metric is satisfied by tracks that never match truth. A silent accuracy collapse on the canonical crossing/overtaking geometry ships uncaught (no other test hard-gates crossing/overtaking accuracy — test_filter_comparison Crossing and test_mht_comparison CrossingWithDropout are print-only with zero assertions).
findings.suggested_test: Add EXPECT_LT(r.mean_ospa, <tight-threshold>) to both tests (parallel_targets already does this), and/or assert that each truth was matched within cutoff for a minimum fraction of steps so a >cutoff drift cannot pass as zero id-switches.

verdict.reasoning: Verified all claim components against the tree. (1) countIdSwitches (core/scenario/Metrics.cpp:9-30) is degenerate exactly as described: when no track is within cutoff it sets best_id=0, counts no switch, and does not update last[i], so permanent drift >cutoff reads as 0 switches. (2) test_crossing.cpp:33-34 and test_overtaking.cpp:33-34 assert only mgr.size()==2u plus countIdSwitches; r.mean_ospa is discarded. (3) I grepped every use of buildCrossingTargetsScenario/buildOvertakingScenario across tests/ and read each: FilterComparison.Crossing asserts only count+id_switches (mean_ospa fprintf-only); MhtComparison.CrossingWithDropout is entirely print-only; BenchRunner JPDA test asserts only track presence (>50); test_reorder_buffer_e2e uses the builder for determinism not accuracy. The only absolute OSPA crossing gate is PmbmScenario.TwoTargetsCrossingTracked (EXPECT_LT mean_ospa<25.0), but that is the PMBM tracker on a different geometry — it does not cover the canonical EKF+GnnAssociator pipeline. BusRegression.Crossing/Overtaking gate mean_ospa only as a ratio (r < baseline*5.01) against a baseline recomputed from the same pipeline, which co-inflates and saturates at cutoff under an accuracy collapse, so it does not hard-gate absolute accuracy. Therefore an EKF/GNN estimator/association regression that keeps two tracks alive but drives them off truth on the crossing/overtaking geometry (classic GNN association degeneracy at the crossing point) ships uncaught. Minor caveat: a broad estimator collapse would also break the straight-line/parallel absolute OSPA gates (FilterComparison.SingleStraightLine <15, ParallelTargets <20), so only a geometry-specific crossing/overtaking regression truly escapes — but that is a real, decision-relevant failure mode, so the gap holds.

findings.title: Birth-existence-target out-of-range fallback test is fully vacuous when no Bernoulli is born
findings.description: TEST(PmbmTrackerUpdate, BirthExistenceTargetOutOfRangeFallsBackToLambdaBirth) sets birth_existence_target=1.5 (invalid) and expects the scalar lambda_birth fallback r=lambda_birth/(lambda_birth+lambda_C). Every assertion is gated by iterating tracker.density().mbm -> h.bernoullis and by the `if (any_bernoulli)` guard on the r_max EXPECT_NEAR. There is no ASSERT that any Bernoulli was actually born (no ASSERT_FALSE(mbm.empty()), no ASSERT_TRUE(any_bernoulli)). If a regression in the out-of-range guard causes the birth path to emit nothing, both the isfinite check and the r_expected check are skipped and the test passes green.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the dedicated guard test for a non-finite/negative-ratio poisoning bug (comment says it previously silently poisoned the Bernoulli). Its whole value is proving the fallback still BIRTHS a Bernoulli at the correct existence; a birth that produces zero Bernoullis is exactly the regression it should catch, yet it passes vacuously.
findings.suggested_test: Add ASSERT_TRUE(any_bernoulli) (or ASSERT_FALSE(tracker.density().mbm.empty()) plus a non-empty-bernoullis assert) before/after the loop so a no-birth regression turns the test red.

findings.title: Alt-birth 'no fresh birth in gated alt children' test skips all assertions if no gated-alt hypothesis exists
findings.description: In test_pmbm_phase8.cpp:897-905 the loop over tracker.density().mbm does `if (h.log_weight >= top_lw - 0.5) continue;` and only then runs EXPECT_NE(b.birth_time.seconds(), scan_t) on the inner Bernoullis. The outer ASSERT_FALSE(mbm.empty()) guarantees hypotheses exist, but NOT that any hypothesis is below the top-0.5 gate. If a regression collapses alt-hypothesis generation (all hypotheses within 0.5 log-weight of the top, or only the top hypothesis survives), every iteration hits `continue` and the fresh-birth assertion never executes -> vacuous pass.
findings.why_it_matters: The test exists to prove gated alt children never carry a fresh birth (the alt_birth_log_gap_threshold contract). A regression that stops producing gated alts altogether is a real failure of that machinery and would be masked.
findings.suggested_test: Count the gated-alt hypotheses inspected and assert that count > 0 after the loop (or construct the scenario so a gated alt is guaranteed and assert its presence).

findings.title: Output-determinism hash truncates metric values to ostringstream default precision (~6 significant digits)
findings.description: hashRows() streams each MetricRow.value into a std::ostringstream with default formatting (os << r.value), i.e. ~6 significant digits, then hashes the string. This is the closest test to the CLAUDE.md 'replay of a log -> byte-identical output' invariant at the metrics level. A non-determinism that changes a metric below the 6th significant figure hashes identical and passes. It also covers only a single config (configs.front(), ekf_cv_gnn) and a single scenario (defaultSimScenarios().front()).
findings.why_it_matters: A small floating-point non-determinism (thread/order/associativity) in the tracker that has not yet amplified past 6 sig figs would slip through the determinism gate the repo relies on; and the check is exercised on only one of the strategy configs.
findings.suggested_test: Stream values with full precision (e.g. std::setprecision(17)) or compare doubles directly, and extend the sweep to more than one config so the determinism guarantee covers the pluggable hot-path strategies.

findings.title: Several estimator/associator comparison TESTs contain zero assertions (print-only, can never fail)
findings.description: test_filter_comparison.cpp ShortRangePass(129), VeryShortRangePass(158), BearingOnlyPass(211), ManeuveringTarget(240), Maneuvering3ModeIMM(280), ShortRangeMultiSeedSweep(334), BearingOnlyMovingSensor(391); plus test_jpda_comparison.cpp:42, test_mht_comparison.cpp:53, and all four test_multi_seed_sweep.cpp cases only fprintf metrics to stderr with no EXPECT/ASSERT. They are documented as comparison/reporting tools (metrics harness), so this is largely by design, but they run as gtest cases that pass unconditionally even if a filter produces NaN/diverged OSPA on these exact configurations.
findings.why_it_matters: These are the only tests exercising some of these specific hard scenarios (e.g. bearing-only moving-sensor PF, 3-mode IMM maneuvering) through the full run pipeline; a divergence unique to those configurations would not be caught here. Underlying estimators are unit-tested elsewhere, which limits severity.
findings.suggested_test: Add a minimal sanity gate (finite mean_ospa and mean_ospa < cutoff for the winning filter) so a diverged run on these scenarios fails rather than silently prints.

units.coverage_notes:
WELL COVERED (good news for synthesis): (1) Sim-bus INPUT determinism is exact — tests/sim/test_bus_determinism.cpp uses EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ on every measurement/truth value and asserts different seeds differ. (2) PMBM output determinism is exactly asserted (test_pmbm_tracker_update.cpp ReplayDeterministicallyReproducesState: EXPECT_EQ on Bernoulli ids + EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ on existence — though not on kinematic mean/cov). (3) Unsorted-batch == sorted-batch equivalence is locked in for both MHT and PMBM with EXPECT_FALSE(empty) guards (tests/pipeline/test_batch_ordering.cpp). (4) ReorderBuffer ordering/late-drop is precisely asserted. (5) AIS-dropout continuity+ID stability, and parallel-targets accuracy (mean_ospa<20) are properly gated. (6) OSPA/GOSPA kernels are asserted against hand-computed constants at 1e-12 (test_ospa.cpp, test_gospa.cpp) — not self-comparisons. (7) Adapter edge validation (out-of-range lat/lon rejection, NaN) is asserted with EXPECT_FALSE. (8) The philos close_approach_labels KEEP_MIXED loop is protected by EXPECT_EQ(keep_mixed_seen,2) so it is NOT vacuous despite the filtered loop.

No DISABLED_ tests exist. All GTEST_SKIP paths are fixture-absence guards (fixtures ARE present in this main tree, so they run here); the only non-fixture skips are benchmark/test_los_guard_haxr_ab.cpp:111 and test_veto_isolation_sim_ab.cpp:57 ('scenario did not generate') and test_rbad_scenario_run.cpp (empty measurements) — could mask a scenario-generation regression as a silent skip, worth a glance but not a hard finding. The 4 EXPECT_NO_THROW uses are all paired with a subsequent value assertion (construct-then-check), so none are throw-only.

COULD NOT DETERMINE: I did not build/run the suite (read-only), so I did not confirm which fixture-gated replay tests actually execute vs skip in this tree, nor observe runtime values behind the loose-looking thresholds. The R-BAD EXPECT_GT(total_our_tracks,0) floor (test_rbad_scenario_run.cpp:122) is a deliberate non-gating consistency/reporting arm per project notes ('consistency not accuracy, no tuning'), so I did not report it as a defect.

findings.title: No mechanism catches a fixture-gated test that silently skipped when it should have run
findings.description: Every scenario/replay/benchmark test uses the pattern `if (!fileExists(...)) GTEST_SKIP()`. There is NO test, script, CI step, or CTest fixture that asserts the expected set of tests actually RAN (not skipped): no `.github/`, no `ci/`, no shell scripts, no `set_tests_properties`/labels, and no tracked expected-skip manifest. gtest_discover_tests + `ctest` reports `100% tests passed` when a skip-guarded scenario or invariant test skips, because GTEST_SKIP counts as pass. The only 'guard' is a human diffing the skip count against prose in docs/algorithms/evaluation-log.md (e.g. 'Suite 1089 ran / 8 skipped'), which is not machine-checked.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the exact failure that hid a RED master for a day (2026-07-08, per evaluation-log.md lines 262-266 and CLAUDE.md feedback_verify_skip_list). A load-bearing scenario test can be turned into a silent no-op by a fixture path typo, a renamed fixture, an unset env var, or a loader that returns empty, and nothing in the build/test system flags it. Wrong-track or invariant regressions ship behind a green ctest.
findings.suggested_test: Add a CTest post-step (or a dedicated test) that runs the suite with --gtest_list_tests and compares actually-run vs an explicit checked-in expected-skip allowlist keyed by fixture availability; fail if any test outside the allowlist skipped.

verdict.reasoning:
I attempted to refute the claim by hunting for any machine-checked mechanism that asserts the expected set of fixture-gated tests actually RAN. I found none.

Where I searched and what I found:
- Filesystem: no `.github/` and no `ci/` directory exist (ls of repo root; targeted find for *.yml/*.yaml/*ci* returned only unrelated paths and the vendored Stone-Soup `.circleci`/`.codecov.yml` under data/, which govern that third-party repo, not navtracker).
- CTest properties: grep across all CMakeLists.txt/*.cmake for `set_tests_properties`, `LABELS`, `FIXTURES_REQUIRED`, `FIXTURES_SETUP`, `WILL_FAIL`, `SKIP_RETURN_CODE`, `REQUIRED_FILES` -> zero hits. Test registration is bare: `enable_testing()` + `gtest_discover_tests(navtracker_tests)` (CMakeLists.txt:22,392) with no extra args, plus one `add_test(CoreOnlyLinks ...)`. gtest_discover_tests with no SKIP handling means a GTEST_SKIP result is reported as a pass by ctest.
- Skip pattern confirmed load-bearing: 48 GTEST_SKIP sites across tests/replay, tests/benchmark, tests/land, etc. Example tests/replay/test_haxr_ospa.cpp:68-72 does `if (!fileExists(...)) GTEST_SKIP()` on a hardcoded relative fixture path — a path typo/rename/missing fixture silently turns the OSPA assertion into a no-op that ctest counts as passed.
- No expected-ran/expected-skip manifest: grep for `expected.skip|skip.manifest|must.run|expected.ran|tests ran|ran /|assert.*not.*skip|skip.*count` across tests/, tools/, docs/, CMakeLists.txt -> only unrelated hits (measurement-builder `expected` vars, source-level "SkipsUngated" behavior tests, docs prose). None is a meta-check on skip counts.
- No tooling parses ctest/gtest output for skips: grep of tools/ and docs/superpowers/ for `SKIPPED|GTEST_SKIP|--gtest_list|ctest` returned only false positives; the one tools/ hit (autoferry_q_calibration.py) is about turn-rates skipped below MIN_SPEED_FOR_HEADING, not test skips. The only `.sh` files under the tree are fixture-extraction scripts (tests/fixtures/philos/batch_extract.sh, pohang_lidar/run_and_cleanup.sh) and vendored venv site-packages — none audit test execution.
- No pytest conftest gating that would fail-not-skip.

The only "guard" is the human-facing convention captured in CLAUDE.md (feedback_verify_skip_list) and docs/algorithms/evaluation-log.md prose (diffing the skip count) — explicitly not machine-checked, which is exactly what the claim states. This is the documented failure that hid a red master for a day (2026-07-08). A load-bearing scenario/invariant test can be silently converted to a no-op by a fixture path typo, rename, unset env, or empty-returning loader, and nothing in the build/test system flags it; wrong-track or invariant regressions can ship behind a green ctest. The auditor did not miss existing coverage — no such coverage exists.

findings.title: sim-multisensor / imazu / rbad / haxr scenario gates resolve fixtures cwd-relative, so plain `ctest` skips them even though the fixtures are present in the tree
findings.description: Unlike the philos tests, which anchor fixtures absolutely via NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR (tests/replay/PhilosLabelReplay.hpp:143 `srcDir()`), the sim-ms/imazu/rbad/haxr scenario loaders default to a CWD-RELATIVE path: `envOr("SIMMS_DIR", "tests/fixtures/sim_multisensor")` (SimMultisensorScenarioRun.cpp:136), `envOr("RBAD_DIR", "tests/fixtures/rbad")` (RbadScenarioRun.cpp:119), and test_haxr_ospa.cpp:39 `"tests/fixtures/haxr_cfar/out/..."`. `ctest`/gtest_discover_tests runs from the build directory, so those relative paths miss and generate() returns empty -> the tests GTEST_SKIP with 'unreachable (set SIMMS_DIR/RBAD_DIR)', even though tests/fixtures/{sim_multisensor,rbad,haxr_cfar} are present in the source tree. The source comments confirm this: 'Skips under ctest (cwd = build/, fixtures unreachable) unless SIMMS_DIR is set' (test_sim_multisensor_scenario_run.cpp:73).
findings.why_it_matters: The silently-skipped set includes the FIRST controlled multi-sensor fusion accuracy gate (SimMultisensorScenarioRun.HeadonFusionAccuracyGate), the ADR-0002 'never suppressed into nothing' invariant (SimMultisensorScenarioRun.AnchoredAndCameraOnlyNeverInvisible), and replay determinism (SimMultisensorScenarioRun.DeterministicReplay, Imazu22ScenarioRun.DeterministicReplaySampled, RbadScenarioRun.DeterministicReplay). Because running these requires an operator-exported env var that a naive `ctest` invocation does not set, the default run turns the fusion and ADR-0002 invariant gates into no-ops. Combined with the missing skip-guard (finding above), a fusion-accuracy or ADR-0002 regression ships with a green suite.
findings.suggested_test: Default these loaders to the absolute NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR-anchored fixture path (as the philos tests do) with the env var as an override, so the committed/generated fixtures are found under `ctest` without external state.

verdict.reasoning: Every link in the claim verified. The sim-ms/imazu/rbad/haxr loaders default to CWD-relative fixture paths (SimMultisensorScenarioRun.cpp:136 `envOr("SIMMS_DIR","tests/fixtures/sim_multisensor")`; RbadScenarioRun.cpp:119 `envOr("RBAD_DIR","tests/fixtures/rbad")`; test_haxr_ospa.cpp:39-41 `"tests/fixtures/haxr_cfar/..."` and `"data/dlr/..."`). By contrast the philos loader anchors absolutely via NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR (PhilosLabelReplay.hpp:143 srcDir()), which the affected loaders never use. CMakeLists.txt:392 registers tests with `gtest_discover_tests(navtracker_tests)` — no WORKING_DIRECTORY and no ENVIRONMENT set, so tests execute from the build directory, where the CWD-relative paths miss; there is no file(COPY)/configure_file/symlink staging fixtures into the build tree. On a miss, generate() returns empty and the tests GTEST_SKIP — this includes HeadonFusionAccuracyGate (test_sim_multisensor_scenario_run.cpp:117-118, the first controlled fusion-accuracy gate), AnchoredAndCameraOnlyNeverInvisible (:149-150, the ADR-0002 never-suppressed-into-nothing invariant), and DeterministicReplay (:178-179) plus the Imazu/Rbad determinism variants. The source comments confirm the behavior ("Skips under ctest (cwd = build/, fixtures unreachable) unless SIMMS_DIR is set"). I searched tests/, .github/, CMakePresets.json, CTestConfig*, conanfile*, Makefile*, scripts/, tools/ for SIMMS_DIR/RBAD_DIR/HAXR_DIR/WORKING_DIRECTORY/ENVIRONMENT and for any reachability/no-skip guard test; found none. The env vars appear only in operator-run baseline docs, not in any CI or default build/test path. No test would catch a regression in these fusion/ADR-0002/determinism gates under a default ctest invocation.

findings.title: No per-test TIMEOUT on any test; a hang has no watchdog
findings.description: gtest_discover_tests is called with no DISCOVERY_TIMEOUT/PROPERTIES override and no `set_tests_properties(... TIMEOUT ...)` exists anywhere (grep for timeout/set_tests_properties across root and bench CMake returns nothing but a LINKER_LANGUAGE line). CTest's default per-test timeout is effectively unlimited. The scenario/replay/benchmark tests iterate over fixture-driven data and CSV/JSON loaders.
findings.why_it_matters: No concrete infinite-loop path was identified, so this is a latent-risk gap rather than an active bug: a loader edge branch that loops on a malformed-but-present fixture, or a sweep that grows unexpectedly, would hang `ctest` indefinitely (notably in an unattended CI run) with no automatic failure. Cheap insurance for a large scenario suite.
findings.suggested_test: Set a generous global CTest TIMEOUT (e.g. via gtest_discover_tests PROPERTIES TIMEOUT or a default set_tests_properties) so a hung test fails rather than blocking the run.

findings.title: Foxglove tests (incl. recorder determinism) are dropped, not skipped, when NAVTRACKER_BUILD_FOXGLOVE=OFF
findings.description: The five foxglove tests (test_geometry, test_foxglove_json, test_mcap_writer, test_recorder, test_recorder_determinism) are added to navtracker_tests only inside `if(NAVTRACKER_BUILD_FOXGLOVE)`. Building with -DNAVTRACKER_BUILD_FOXGLOVE=OFF removes them from the binary entirely (not a GTEST_SKIP) with no signal, and no other test covers the recorder determinism/serialization invariant in that config.
findings.why_it_matters: Default is ON so the normal build covers them, but a consumer or CI that turns Foxglove off gets a green suite that silently exercises none of the recorder path, including its determinism guarantee. Failure mode is limited to the debug-recorder adapter, hence low.
findings.suggested_test: Document the FOXGLOVE=OFF build as an intentionally reduced-coverage config, or emit a CMake message/marker test recording that the foxglove suite was excluded.

units.coverage_notes:
WELL COVERED / good news: (1) Zero orphan tests — `comm -23` of `git ls-files tests/*.cpp` against test paths referenced in CMakeLists.txt is empty; all ~190 tracked test .cpp are compiled into navtracker_tests (plus tests/types/test_link_core_only.cpp as the separate CoreOnlyLinks executable). No silently-dead test files. (2) Parallel-safe (-j): no test writes into the source tree; temp writers use $TMPDIR / ::testing::TempDir(). TmpPath.hpp suffixes with PID+atomic counter; test_ais_csv_replay_adapter and test_autoferry_json_replay use per-test-unique filenames/labels, so no cross-test path collision under `ctest -j`. (3) The CLAUDE.md determinism invariant is covered by ALWAYS-ON synthetic tests that never fixture-gate: BenchDeterminism.RepeatedSweepProducesIdenticalRows, SimulatedSensorBusDeterminism.TwoRunsSameSeedIdenticalOutput, RecorderDeterminism.SameInputIdenticalPayloads. (4) philos/haxr LABEL replay tests anchor fixtures absolutely via NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR (PhilosLabelReplay.hpp srcDir()), so with fixtures present they RUN under plain ctest — the correct pattern (contrast finding 2). (5) Config-struct drift is mechanically enforced by tests/docs/test_integration_guide_config_coverage.cpp. (6) bench/CMakeLists.txt registers only executables (no add_test), so nothing there is an unregistered ctest test.

FIXTURE-SKIP INVENTORY (all GTEST_SKIP-gated cases): absolute-anchored (RUN when fixtures present in tree): tests/replay/test_philos_* (farcross, ospa, sunset_labels, close_approach_labels, close_approach_cpa, los_shadow_probe, occupancy_coverage_6c), test_camera_bearing_loader smoke, test_geojson_coastline BostonFixtureSmoke, test_radar_truth_loader, test_autoferry_json_replay LoadsRealScenario2 (data/autoferry present). CWD-RELATIVE/env-gated (SKIP under plain ctest from build/ despite fixtures present — see finding 2): test_haxr_ospa.KattwykHourEkfGnnBaseline, test_sim_multisensor_scenario_run.* (Generate…, AisMeasurementsCarryMmsiHint, HeadonFusionAccuracyGate, AnchoredAndCameraOnlyNeverInvisible=ADR-0002, DeterministicReplay), test_imazu22_scenario_run.* (4), test_rbad_scenario_run.* (3), test_clutter_burst_birth_confirm_probe.Race, test_philos_cluttermap_ab, test_replay_scenario_run.* (5), test_los_guard_haxr_ab, test_veto_isolation_haxr_ab, test_veto_isolation_sim_ab. Truly data-gated (data/ absent in this tree, e.g. data/haxr): the HAXR data-path variants.

COULD NOT DETERMINE: exact runtime skip count/names — the tree is read-only and I did not build or run ctest; the eval-log 'ran 1089 / 8 skipped' figures imply the team's verified runs export SIMMS_DIR (and skip only data/-gated HAXR/RBAD/Boston), which is consistent with finding 2's fragility but I could not reproduce it.
